requirements_register.text: Obtain name, date of birth, address, and identification number before opening an account.
Verify identity within a reasonable time using documentary or non-documentary methods.
Retain customer identification records for five years after the account is closed.
Apply enhanced due diligence to accounts rated high risk under the enterprise risk model.

clauses.heading: Purpose
clauses.text: This policy establishes the Customer Identification Program used to verify the identity of customers opening accounts.

clauses.heading: Scope
clauses.text: This policy applies to all lines of business that open accounts for customers in the United States.

clauses.heading: Identifying information
clauses.text: Before opening an account, the bank shall obtain the customer's name, date of birth, residential or business address, and identification number.

clauses.heading: Identity verification
clauses.text: The bank shall verify each customer's identity within a reasonable time after account opening using documentary or non-documentary methods.

clauses.heading: Recordkeeping
clauses.text: Customer identification records shall be retained for five years after the account is closed.

clauses.heading: High-risk customers
clauses.text: Enhanced due diligence shall be applied to customers rated high risk under the enterprise risk model.

clauses.heading: Roles and responsibilities
clauses.text: The BSA Officer owns this policy. Line-of-business managers are responsible for operational execution.

clauses.heading: Exceptions
clauses.text: Exceptions to this policy must be approved in writing by the BSA Officer and logged in the exception register.

prior_clauses.text: This policy establishes the Customer Identification Program used to verify the identity of customers opening accounts.
This policy applies to all lines of business that open accounts for customers in the United States.
Before opening an account, the bank shall obtain the customer's name, date of birth, and identification number.
The bank shall verify each customer's identity within a reasonable time after account opening using documentary or non-documentary methods.
Customer identification records shall be retained for five years after the account is closed.
The BSA Officer owns this policy. Line-of-business managers are responsible for operational execution.
